examples: I felt so awkward at the party.
It was awkward meeting my ex at the store.
context: I think it's wrong. This is awkward. This sounds really unnatural.

examples: I'm definitely coming to the party.
This is definitely the best pizza I've ever had.
You should definitely try the cheesecake.
context: It's not as important. I feel like in Korea, the stakes are definitely much higher and it's more intense.
I was to my Kamalade. Plus, I'm a native English speaker, so I think that definitely is working in my favor.

examples: She literally ran a marathon yesterday.
The store is literally around the corner.
context: I literally just showed up to my test and took it.

examples: I was struggling to keep up with the pace.
Many students are struggling with math.
She's been struggling financially this year.
context: I'm struggling. I can't imagine a high school student having to do that.
